top_signals.title: Christine Lagarde leaves door open to early ECB exit, as she mulls French politics
top_signals.summary: The European Central Bank's Christine Lagarde has declined to rule out an early end to her term as president, as she mulls a foray into French politics.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Bank of England launches policy statement and draft rules on regulating systemic stablecoins
top_signals.summary: The Bank of England has today published its policy statement and draft Code of Practice (rules) for systemic stablecoin issuers.

top_signals.title: Venture Capital During the AI Revolution: Masters in Business with Mamoon Hamid
top_signals.summary: Barry sits down with Kleiner Perkins Partner, Mamoon Hamid. They discuss Mamoon's thoughts on the AI revolution and his approach to early AI investing.  Mamoon also breaks down how he became an early investor in giants like Slack, Figma, and how Kleiner Perkins assesses the investments they missed. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Japan’s princesses face life-altering legal revisions
top_signals.summary: After decades of discussion over their future, the princesses may soon have the option to either leave or remain in the imperial family.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: TotalEnergies Offers Millions of Barrels of Iraqi Crude to Asian Buyers
top_signals.summary: TotalEnergies is offering millions of barrels of Iraq’s Basrah Medium and Basrah Heavy crudes for prompt delivery to Asia this month and next, traders who had received some of these offers told Bloomberg on Friday. Iraq, one of the Persian Gulf producers most hit by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, has been offering crude on the spot market with deep discounts and only on a loading basis, which means buyers need to secure empty tankers to move inbound into the Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz. But TotalEnergies, one of the top commodity…

top_signals.title: Google loses final appeal to overturn €4.1 billion EU fine
top_signals.summary: Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) has dismissed Google's final appeal against a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) antitrust fine over the company's use of Android to promote its Chrome browser and search service. [...]

top_signals.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
top_signals.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total energy use.
